seoTitle: How to Create a New Jira Project
metaDescription: Set up a brand new Jira project step by step, from choosing a template to inviting your team.
intro: Starting a new Jira project means making a few upfront decisions about template, key, and access that shape how the project runs going forward. This guide covers creating a project from the ground up.

steps.heading: Start the create project flow
steps.body: From the Jira homepage or projects menu, choose the option to create a new project.

steps.heading: Pick a template
steps.body: Select a template that matches your team's process, such as Scrum, Kanban, or a service management template.

steps.heading: Set the project name and key
steps.body: Enter a name and confirm the short project key, which will prefix every issue created in the project.

steps.heading: Configure access and permissions
steps.body: Decide who can view and edit the project, adjusting permission schemes if needed.

steps.heading: Invite your team
steps.body: Add team members to the project so they can start creating and working on issues right away.

faq.question: Can I change the project key after creating the project?
faq.answer: Changing the project key is possible but limited, and it can affect existing issue links, so it's best chosen carefully upfront.

faq.question: What's the difference between a team-managed and company-managed project?
faq.answer: Team-managed projects are simpler and self-contained, while company-managed projects share schemes and configurations across the organization.

faq.question: Do I need admin rights to create a new project?
faq.answer: Depending on your Jira site's settings, project creation may be open to all users or restricted to administrators.

faq.question: Can I switch templates after the project is created?
faq.answer: Not directly; switching board type (like Kanban to Scrum) is possible in some setups, but full template changes usually require a new project.
